2010-11-01.title: In a First, Brazil Elects a Woman as President
2010-11-01.abstract: In choosing Dilma Rousseff, Brazilians voted strongly in favor of continuing the economic and social policies of the popular president, Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva.

2010-11-01.abstract: Sarah E. Shourd, a teacher freed in September after nearly 14 months in Tehran’s Evin prison says the group inadvertently crossed into Iran.

2010-11-01.abstract: The incumbent president, Laurent Gbagbo, 65, whose term ended in 2005, clung to power amid rebellions and disputes.

2010-11-01.abstract: Twenty-one years ago, Antonio Meño Ortega, entered a coma after being accidentally deprived of oxygen while undergoing cosmetic surgery. His parents have fought a public legal battle.

2010-11-03.abstract: A wave of explosive devices sent across Europe unnerved authorities, who are already trying to assess risks to the air cargo system.

2010-11-03.abstract: While the exercises were held amid increased tensions between China and its neighbors, one Chinese military analyst called the war games routine.

2010-11-04.abstract: A French court on Wednesday ordered the extradition of a Rwandan rebel leader to the International Criminal Court in The Hague on charges of raping and killing civilians in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

2010-11-06.abstract: Mr. Ban said that China was on the path to political reform and building a society based on the rule of law.

2010-11-28.title: South Korea Experiences a Stirring for Revenge
2010-11-28.abstract: The ferocity of the attack on Yeonpyeong Island appears to have started a shift in South Koreans’ conflicted emotions about their countrymen in the North.

2010-11-28.title: Haiti Election Marred by ID Card Problems
2010-11-28.abstract: Violence has been light, but the aftermath of January’s earthquake may create confusion in Sunday’s election.

2010-11-28.title: Iraq: Moments of Release
2010-11-28.abstract: Ordinary Iraqis trying to go about their lives.

2010-11-29.title: For City Dwellers, a Taste of the Orderly Life
2010-11-29.abstract: In a nation where runaway urban growth has caused all manner of ills, the developers of CitraLand, in Surabaya, Indonesia, have looked to Singapore for the answers.

2010-11-29.title: Calls From Man With Terror Links Prompted German Alert, Official Says
2010-11-29.abstract: Germany’s recent decision to declare a terrorism alert was set off by phone calls from a man who warned of a pending attack, according to a law enforcement official.

2010-11-29.title: Catalonia Vote Reflects Desire for Less Obligation to Madrid
2010-11-29.abstract: The victory of a Catalan nationalist party could heighten regional tensions and further challenge Spain’s prime minister.

2010-11-29.title: Fraud Charges Mar Egypt Vote
2010-11-29.abstract: There was a sense among many that Egypt’s long-dominant party was bent on entrenching its hold.

2010-11-29.title: Chaos at the Polls in Haiti
2010-11-29.abstract: In many places, voters in Haiti’s first presidential election since the January earthquake encountered confusion, delays and intimidation, along with accusations of fraud.

2010-11-30.title: Global Climate Change Talks Begin in Cancún
2010-11-30.abstract: After last year’s Copenhagen conference failed to produce agreement on climate change, hopes are that small steps can be made in Mexico.

2010-11-30.title: Anticorruption Group Claims Harassment in Pakistan
2010-11-30.abstract: The Pakistani chapter of Transparency International says government officials have intimidated and harassed the group over its monitoring of American aid.

2010-11-30.title: On Iraqi Television, Crying Out for the Missing
2010-11-30.abstract: The Iraqis who call in to “Patron of the Oppressed” describe how searches of prisons, police stations, hospitals and morgues have left them with no clues to loved ones’ whereabouts.

2010-11-30.title: Hoping to Avoid Bombs and Win Afghan Minds
2010-11-30.abstract: For American troops in a heavily mined village, avoiding improvised explosive devices is the most difficult task.

2010-11-30.title: A Footstep, Then an Explosion and an Urgent Call: ‘Medic!’
2010-11-30.abstract: An account of how a photographer was wounded by a mine illustrates the risks that soldiers and journalists face on even the most careful patrols for explosives.
